title: os and arch support to inspect command
body:
In the meantime, the only of select the proper digest of the multi-arch image according to the given os and arch is using the `jq` utility. We (w/@dentrax) tried to use `override-os` and `override-arch` arguments to make it work, but it didn't work. So, we thought that it'd be better to support these parameters for working multi-arch images without the need for any tool.

```shell
$ export OS=linux ARCH=amd64
$ skopeo inspect docker://devopps/alpine:3.16 --raw | jq ".manifests | map(select(.platform.architecture == \"${ARCH}\" and .platform.os == \"${OS}\") )" | jq -r '.Digest'
```

This one above would become this one below:

```shell
$ skopeo inspect --override-os linux --override-arch amd64 docker://devopps/alpine:3.16 --raw | jq -r '.Digest'
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

As discussed in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1689#issuecomment-1165926970 , it would be unexpected for `--override-os` to change the fundamental behavior of an operation.

The more generic RFE for `skopeo inspect` to list to show per-arch-arch manifest digest values is a RFE tracked in #1283 (all values) or #1554 (current architecture).

Is there another aspect to this that should be tracked separately?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
